checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: Created docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md with: stable-contract language ('you can hard-code providerId values...We treat a breaking ID change the same way we'd treat a breaking API change'), a deprecation cycle that requires both old and new IDs served concurrently for at least one minor release cycle, a CHANGELOG expectation ('Every addition, deprecation, or removal is recorded in the CHANGELOG under a Providers heading'), readable integrator-focused prose, and links from both README.md and docs/API.md. No code changes — correct for a docs deliverable.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Exactly three documentation-only files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new, the primary deliverable), README.md (one-line link addition), docs/API.md (one-line 'See also' link). No other files touched; no source code edits.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: The new file is 85 lines of substantive, real documentation including a canonical provider-IDs table, a three-phase deprecation cycle, a stability contract section, and actionable integrator guidance. No placeholder/empty text. No tests were modified — verification passed on docs-only changes.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: Documentation is accurate and describes a reasonable, well-scoped stability contract and deprecation policy. No misleading claims, no security-sensitive information exposed, no correctness risks. Safe to surface in a PR.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence

No code changes — correct for a docs deliverable.

Exactly three documentation-only files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new, the primary deliverable), README.md (one-line link addition), docs/API.md (one-line 'See also' link). No other files touched; no source code edits.

The new file is 85 lines of substantive, real documentation including a canonical provider-IDs table, a three-phase deprecation cycle, a stability contract section, and actionable integrator guidance. No placeholder/empty text. No tests were modified — verification passed on docs-only changes.
